documentedClaim.statement: Duplicate names are not assumed to override or merge; preserve both discovered sources unless the versioned adapter proves otherwise.
documentedClaim.citation: docs/ontology/README.md §6.2 (Codex Skills row), citing https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/build-skills. See also docs/knowledge/README.md §4's illustrative Knowledge Pack example, which names a 'codex.skills.discovery' precedence program with operator 'KEEP_BOTH' for this exact concept -- NOTE: 'KEEP_BOTH' is not itself one of the nine closed merge operators docs/ontology/README.md §3.1 / internal/ontology/mergeoperator.go define, so this fixture maps the documented claim onto the closest defined operator, NAMESPACE ('duplicate names remain distinct through a package or directory prefix'), rather than reusing the undefined 'KEEP_BOTH' term; that mapping itself is this fixture's own judgment call, not an additional confirmed fact.

entries.reason: Three sources define a skill named 'deploy': $HOME/.agents/skills (documented), the project's .agents/skills (documented), and $CODEX_HOME/skills -- a discovery root NOT listed in docs/ontology/README.md §6.2's physical-mapping table for Codex Skills, but found by static inspection (strings) of the installed codex-cli 0.144.5 native binary referencing '$CODEX_HOME/skills' as a skill install/discovery location (see fixtures/README.md). This is a genuine discrepancy between the documented ontology claim and this pass's own static evidence, itself worth flagging rather than silently reconciling. Whether the real host actually loads/offers all three simultaneously, silently drops any of them, or disambiguates by some order this pass has not observed is UNKNOWN: no safe non-interactive introspection path exists to dump Codex's resolved skill catalog.
